# Helpers functions

These are some helper functions that are needed for the posterior computation

These functions are not documented for now. (TODO: do this).

<!-- send_to to put all helper functions into one file.. -->

```{r, send_to = 'R/all-helpers.R'}
#' Compute the function A(kappa) = I_{p/2}(kappa) / I_{p/2-1}(kappa)
#' for the von Mises–Fisher distribution.
#' @param kappa concentration parameter (non‑negative scalar)
#' @param p space dimension (default = 2)
#' @return scalar value of A(kappa)
#' @export
get_Ap <- function(kappa, p = 2) {
  if (kappa < 1e-6) return(0)
  besselI(kappa, p/2, expon.scaled = TRUE) /
    besselI(kappa, p/2 - 1, expon.scaled = TRUE)
}

#' Compute the score function s(eta, x) for vMF.
#' @param eta natural parameter vector (length p)
#' @param x unit vector observation (length p)
#' @param p space dimension (default = 2)
#' @return score vector of length p
#' @export
score_fn <- function(eta, x, p = 2) {
  kappa <- sqrt(sum(eta^2))
  if (kappa < 1e-6) return(x)
  expected <- get_Ap(kappa, p) * eta / kappa
  x - expected
}


#' Fisher information matrix for the vMF model
#'
#' @param C  natural parameter vector (length p)
#' @param p  dimension (defaults to length(C))
#' @return   p x p Fisher information matrix
#' @export
get_FIM_vmf <- function(C, p = length(C)) {
  kappa <- sqrt(sum(C^2))
  I_p <- diag(p)
  if (kappa < 1e-6) return(I_p / p)

  mu <- C / kappa
  Ap <- get_Ap(kappa, p)
  Ap_prime <- 1 - Ap^2 - ((p - 1) / kappa) * Ap

  mu_mu <- mu %*% t(mu)
  Ap_prime * mu_mu + (Ap / kappa) * (I_p - mu_mu)
}


#' Compute the sum of weights sum_{t = start}^{end} 1/(t + N)^2.
#' @param start starting index (t >= 1)
#' @param end ending index
#' @param N number of observed data points
#' @return scalar value of the sum
#' @export
compute_weight_sum <- function(start, end, N) {
  sum(1 / ((start:end) + N)^2)
}
```


```{r, send_to = 'R/all-helpers.R'}
#' Compute MLE given data X 
#' @param X nrow = p ncol = number of observation
#' @return MLE as a p-dim vector
#' @export
mle_vmf <- function(X){
  n <- ncol(X)
  
  sum_vec <- rowSums(X)  # 2-dim vector sum
  Robs <- sqrt(sum(sum_vec^2))
  Rbar <- Robs / n
  mu_hat <- atan2(sum_vec[2], sum_vec[1])
  
  if (Rbar < 0.001) {
      kappa_hat <- 0
    } else if (Rbar > 0.999) {
      kappa_hat <- 1 / (2 * (1 - Rbar))
    } else {
      kappa_hat <- uniroot(function(k) get_Ap(k, 2) - Rbar, c(0.001, 100))$root
    }
  
  c_hat <- kappa_hat * c(cos(mu_hat), sin(mu_hat))
  
  return(c_hat)
}


#' functions for simulations study
#' @param n sample size
#' @param n_sim sim times
#' @param mu_ture,kappa_true true param
#' @param B number of chains
#' @param M number of iteration for each chain
#' @param seed for reproducability
#' @param n_cores number of cores you can use
#' @param save_all save data or not
#' @return all the data with a summary we gonna used
#' @export
sim_martingale_study <- function(n, n_sim = 500,
                                 mu_true = 2, kappa_true = 4,
                                 B = 5000,M = 50,
                                 seed = 1234,
                                 n_cores = 1,
                                 save_all = FALSE) {

  RNGkind("L'Ecuyer-CMRG")
  set.seed(seed)
  
  # ---- Storage ----
  cover_mu <- cover_kappa <- numeric(n_sim)
  len_mu <- len_kappa <- numeric(n_sim)
  mean_mu_vec <- mean_kappa_vec <- numeric(n_sim)
  times <- numeric(n_sim)
  if (save_all) {results <- vector("list", n_sim)}
  
  eta_true <- kappa_true * c(cos(mu_true), sin(mu_true))
  
  
  # ---- Simulation loop ----
  for (i in 1:n_sim) {
    # Generate observations
    X <- replicate(n,rstiefel::rmf.vector(eta_true))
    
    
    # Run B independent chains
    t0 <- proc.time()[3]
    chains<- run_predresamp_vmf(X, B = 5000, M=50,n_cores = n_cores,
                                return_chains = save_all)
    t1 <- proc.time()[3]
    times[i] <- t1 - t0
    
    # Extract samples
    sample_list <- chains$sample
    
    mu_sam <- sample_list$mu_sam
    kappa_sam <- sample_list$kappa_sam
    
    mean_mu_vec[i] <- mean(mu_sam)
    mean_kappa_vec[i] <- mean(kappa_sam)
    
    # 95% credible intervals for mu and kappa
    ci_mu <- quantile(mu_sam, probs = c(0.025, 0.975))
    cover_mu[i] <- (mu_true >= ci_mu[1] && mu_true <= ci_mu[2])
    len_mu[i] <- ci_mu[2] - ci_mu[1]
    
    ci_kappa <- quantile(kappa_sam, c(0.025, 0.975))
    cover_kappa[i] <- (kappa_true >= ci_kappa[1]) & (kappa_true <= ci_kappa[2])
    len_kappa[i] <- ci_kappa[2] - ci_kappa[1]
    
    if (save_all) {results[[i]]<- chains}
    
  }
  
  # ---- Build output table ----
  summarys <- data.frame(
    n = n,
    Coverage_mu = mean(cover_mu) * 100,
    Length_mu = mean(len_mu),
    Coverage_kappa = mean(cover_kappa) * 100,
    Length_kappa = mean(len_kappa),
    Mean_SE_mu      = sd(mean_mu_vec),
    Mean_SE_kappa   = sd(mean_kappa_vec),
    Mean_Time = mean(times)
  )
  
  if (save_all) {
    list(summary = summarys, data_all = results)
  } else {
    summarys
  }
}


```


```{r plot_trace_sideways_density, include=FALSE}
#' Plot MPS traces with an attached sideways density
#'
#' Draws simulated MPS trace paths on the left and attaches the terminal
#' posterior density on the right. The x-axis is drawn only over the trace
#' region, so iteration labels do not extend into the attached density region.
#'
#' @param paths Numeric matrix of trace paths. Rows are paths/chains and columns
#'   are iterations. For example, `res_paths$paths`.
#' @param samples Numeric vector of terminal MPS samples used for the attached
#'   sideways density.
#' @param iter Numeric vector of iteration indices. Defaults to
#'   `0:(ncol(paths) - 1)`.
#' @param ref_value Optional horizontal reference value, such as an MLE or sample
#'   mean.
#' @param ref_density_fun Optional function of `y` returning a reference density
#'   on the same vertical scale, e.g.
#'   `function(y) dnorm(y, mean = mu_hat, sd = sqrt(1 / n))`.
#' @param xlab,ylab,main Axis labels and title.
#' @param density_width_frac Width of the attached density as a fraction of the
#'   trace x-range.
#' @param density_x_offset_frac Starting location of the attached density as a
#'   fraction of the trace x-range.
#' @param xlim_multiplier Total x-range multiplier used to reserve space for the
#'   attached density.
#' @return Invisibly returns a list with the estimated density, y-range,
#'   density x-offset, and density scaling factor.
plot_trace_sideways_density <- function(
  paths,
  samples,
  iter = NULL,
  ref_value = NULL,
  ref_density_fun = NULL,
  xlab = "Forward step i",
  ylab = expression(hat(mu)[n+i]),
  main = NULL,
  trace_col = rgb(0.25, 0.45, 0.75, 0.06),
  trace_lwd = 0.5,
  density_fill = rgb(0.25, 0.45, 0.75, 0.30),
  density_border = rgb(0.20, 0.35, 0.65),
  density_lwd = 1.2,
  ref_col = "red",
  ref_lwd = 1.5,
  ref_lty = 2,
  ref_density_col = "red",
  ref_density_lwd = 2,
  density_width_frac = 0.22,
  density_x_offset_frac = 1.02,
  xlim_multiplier = 1.25,
  y_pad_frac = 0.04,
  cex_lab = 1.2,
  cex_axis = 1.0,
  cex_main = 1.2,
  cex_leg = 0.9,
  legend_pos = "topleft",
  show_legend = TRUE
) {
  if (!is.matrix(paths)) paths <- as.matrix(paths)
  if (!is.numeric(paths)) stop("`paths` must be numeric.")
  if (!is.numeric(samples)) stop("`samples` must be numeric.")

  if (is.null(iter)) iter <- seq_len(ncol(paths)) - 1
  if (length(iter) != ncol(paths)) {
    stop("`iter` must have length equal to `ncol(paths)`.")
  }

  x_start <- min(iter)
  x_end <- max(iter)
  x_width <- x_end - x_start
  if (x_width <= 0) stop("`iter` must span a positive range.")

  y_range <- range(c(as.vector(paths), samples, ref_value), finite = TRUE)
  y_pad <- y_pad_frac * diff(y_range)
  if (!is.finite(y_pad) || y_pad == 0) y_pad <- 0.1
  y_range <- y_range + c(-y_pad, y_pad)

  dens <- density(samples, n = 512)
  y_grid <- seq(y_range[1], y_range[2], length.out = 500)

  ref_density <- NULL
  if (!is.null(ref_density_fun)) ref_density <- ref_density_fun(y_grid)

  dens_scale <- x_width * density_width_frac / max(dens$y, ref_density, na.rm = TRUE)
  x_offset <- x_start + x_width * density_x_offset_frac
  x_max <- x_start + x_width * xlim_multiplier

  plot(
    NULL,
    xlim = c(x_start, x_max),
    ylim = y_range,
    xlab = "",
    ylab = ylab,
    main = main,
    xaxt = "n",
    cex.lab = cex_lab,
    cex.axis = cex_axis,
    cex.main = cex_main
  )

  ticks <- pretty(c(x_start, x_end))
  ticks <- ticks[ticks >= x_start & ticks <= x_end]
  axis(1, at = ticks, labels = ticks, lwd = 0, lwd.ticks = 1, cex.axis = cex_axis)
  usr <- par("usr")
  segments(x_start, usr[3], x_end, usr[3], xpd = FALSE)
  mtext(xlab, side = 1, line = 2.8, at = (x_start + x_end) / 2, cex = cex_lab)

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(paths))) {
    lines(iter, paths[i, ], col = trace_col, lwd = trace_lwd)
  }

  if (!is.null(ref_value)) {
    abline(h = ref_value, col = ref_col, lwd = ref_lwd, lty = ref_lty)
  }

  polygon(
    x_offset + dens$y * dens_scale,
    dens$x,
    col = density_fill,
    border = density_border,
    lwd = density_lwd
  )

  if (!is.null(ref_density)) {
    lines(
      x_offset + ref_density * dens_scale,
      y_grid,
      col = ref_density_col,
      lwd = ref_density_lwd
    )
  }

  segments(x_offset, y_range[1], x_offset, y_range[2],
           col = "gray60", lty = 3, lwd = 0.8)

  if (show_legend) {
    legend(
      legend_pos,
      legend = c("MPS trajectories", "Reference value",
                 "MPS density", "Reference density"),
      col = c(adjustcolor(trace_col, alpha.f = 0.6), ref_col,
              density_border, ref_density_col),
      lty = c(1, ref_lty, 1, 1),
      lwd = c(1.5, ref_lwd, density_lwd, ref_density_lwd),
      pch = c(NA, NA, 15, NA),
      pt.cex = c(NA, NA, 1.5, NA),
      bty = "n",
      cex = cex_leg
    )
  }

  invisible(list(
    density = dens,
    y_range = y_range,
    x_offset = x_offset,
    dens_scale = dens_scale
  ))
}

### Example Usage:
#plot_trace_sideways_density(
#  paths = res_paths$paths,
#  samples = samples,
#  iter = 0:T_iter,
#  ref_value = mu_hat,
#  ref_density_fun = function(y) dnorm(y, mean = mu_hat, sd = sqrt(1 / n)),
#  main = "(a)"
#)
```
